name: Family First Of Ny
description_short: Family First Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative offering banking, mortgages, loans, and savings products with over 50 years of service in New York.
description_long:
Family First Federal Credit Union has operated for over 50 years as a member-focused credit union in New York. The organization recently completed a merger with Focus First Credit Union, expanding its member base and service offerings. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Family First operates on a cooperative model where members are owners, theoretically resulting in better rates and fewer fees compared to for-profit institutions.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs) with competitively raised rates, personal loans, auto loans, student loan options, debt consolidation, mortgage lending (purchase, refinance, home equity, and home improvement loans), and a Homebuyer Dream Program providing first-time homebuyer assistance with funds starting at $10,000. Members can access accounts through online banking, mobile app, bill payment services, mobile check deposit, and digital wallets. The organization also offers a Companion Savings account with 5.00% APY and 1% unlimited cash back on debit transactions.

Family First distinguishes itself through member-centric programs like its Refer-A-Friend initiative (earning up to $250), the Homebuyer Dream Program for first-time buyers, and a stated commitment to high CD rates. The organization maintains physical branch locations including Webster and appears actively engaged in community education through webinars and financial literacy content. The credit union structure inherently offers advantages like potential profit-sharing through dividends and lower fees.

As a regional credit union, Family First's primary limitation is geographic restriction to New York members or those with qualifying connections. While offering competitive rates and products, the organization's resources are smaller than national banks. The website contains limited transparency on specific loan terms, rates for various products, and membership eligibility requirements beyond geographic location, requiring prospective members to contact the institution directly for detailed pricing information.
services: Checking and savings accounts with bundle options
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with competitive rates
Mortgage lending (purchase, refinance, home equity, home improvement)
Homebuyer Dream Program (first-time buyer assistance with $10,000+ funds)
Online and mobile banking with bill payment
Digital wallets and contactless payment options
Companion Savings account (5.00% APY, 1% cash back)
Financial literacy webinars and community education
pros: Companion Savings account offering 5.00% APY with 1% unlimited cash back on debit purchases and no minimum balance requirement
Over 50 years of established history and recent merger expansion through Focus First Credit Union
Homebuyer Dream Program providing up to $10,000 in funds toward first home purchases for qualifying members
Competitive CD rates that have been recently raised to reward member deposits
Comprehensive digital banking including mobile check deposit, secure bill payments, mobile app alerts, and digital wallet support
Member referral program offering up to $250 for successful friend and family referrals
Full-service mortgage lending including purchase, refinance, home equity, and home improvement loan options
cons: Geographic limitation as a regional credit union requiring membership eligibility based on New York location or qualifying connections
Limited rate and product term transparency on the website; prospective members must contact directly for specific pricing
Only one ATM location mentioned (Webster branch), currently unavailable for service, limiting cash access convenience
No information provided about membership fees, account minimums for most products, or annual percentage rates for loans
Relatively small institution compared to national banks, potentially limiting product innovation and technological capabilities
best_for: New York-based consumers seeking competitive savings and CD rates with high-yield accounts
First-time homebuyers interested in specialized assistance programs and mortgage products
Credit union members seeking relationship-based banking with member-ownership benefits and profit-sharing potential
diagnosis: Family First Federal Credit Union is best for New York-area residents seeking competitive savings rates, mortgage services, and first-time homebuyer programs with member-owned institution benefits. The primary caveat is geographic restriction to New York members and limited public transparency on specific loan rates and terms, requiring direct contact with the institution for pricing details.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
